meta.surface: merchant-ops product — catalog, orders, assistant permissions
meta.primary_outcome: fulfill/manage an order while staying oriented in the seller shell
meta.focus: top header chrome — equal-weight persistent UI vs quieter/progressive treatment
meta.evidence_basis: frozen screenshot only; no source, logs, APIs, hidden state, or prior reports

design_thesis: A merchant-ops console should keep destinations loud and status/meta quiet so a shopkeeper can finish an order without the chrome competing for equal attention.

product_specific_design_philosophy.framing: Merchant ops console, not luxury marketplace
principles: Orientation first: brand + current section must answer 'where am I?' in under one glance
Destinations over telemetry: nav paths beat readiness chips
Progressive disclosure for ambient trust: Verified / Ready are confirmations, not peer CTAs
Dense but calm: grocery shopkeepers scan for Orders and the next fulfill action, not a status dashboard in the header
Trust through restraint: fewer equal pills = more credible ops tool

axis_positions.reason: Active Orders pill, Search pill, Assistant status pill, and Identity status pill share similar capsule weight; the eye cannot rank destination vs ambient state.
Brand + 5 nav labels + Search + two verbose status capsules + Sign out sit on one horizontal band with little grouping separation.
Sans is clear; brand bold works; status strings ('Assistant status: Ready', 'Identity status: Verified') read as full labels at nearly nav size rather than secondary meta.
Right cluster packs Search + two status pills with little breathing room; Sign out is the only quiet escape hatch.
One capsule language used for active route, utility Search, and two health indicators — craft is consistent but semantically wrong.
Verified / Ready should reassure; as equal-weight chrome they feel like perpetual alerts rather than settled trust.

first_impression_header: The header reads like a status toolbar wearing a nav costume: brand and Orders are fine, but Search + Assistant Ready + Identity Verified all shout at the same volume as the place you are, so orientation fights telemetry before the Dispatch order button even gets a chance.

findings.title: Status capsules compete with destinations
findings.detail: Assistant status: Ready and Identity status: Verified use the same light-gray pill language (and similar presence) as Search and sit adjacent to the active Orders teal pill. Persistent ambient state is treated as equal-weight chrome to primary navigation.

findings.title: Verbose status copy inflates chrome weight
findings.detail: Full phrases 'Assistant status: Ready' and 'Identity status: Verified' occupy header real estate like destinations. For a shopkeeper on an order detail page, these should be quieter icons/short tokens, not peer labels.

findings.title: Single-rail overload blurs brand → nav → meta grouping
findings.detail: Brand/tagline, five nav items, Search, two statuses, and Sign out share one band without clear progressive zones. The shell fails the 'stay oriented while fulfilling' outcome at the chrome layer.

findings.title: Search styled as peer pill to statuses
findings.detail: Search is a utility control but visually rhymes with status chips; it neither reads as a quiet tool nor as a distinct action, adding to equal-weight noise.

findings.title: Sign out is correctly quiet; contrast proves the rest is too loud
findings.detail: Text-only Sign out is appropriately secondary. That correct quietness highlights how over-present the adjacent status pills are.

screenshot_attestation: I personally Read the screenshot file at the provided path and judged only what is visibly present in that freeze (including browser chrome and Hermes bridge bar as non-product noise, excluded from product findings).

signature_visual_moves.name: Two-tier shell: destinations loud, assurance quiet
signature_visual_moves.direction: Keep brand + nav (active route only) as the sole filled chrome accent; demote Ready/Verified to a compact identity/assurance cluster (icon + short word, or a single 'Verified · Ready' meta line) with no filled pill competition against Orders.

signature_visual_moves.name: Progressive trust, not perpetual badges
signature_visual_moves.direction: Treat Assistant/Identity as settled background trust: muted, right-aligned, expandable on click/hover; reserve capsule energy for the current merchant job (Orders / Dispatch), not for always-green health.

anti_homogenization_notes: Do not solve hierarchy by adding another purple/glow/AI-agent aesthetic — this is a grocery ops console; keep teal as the single accent for jobs-in-progress only.
Avoid turning every header affordance into the same rounded pill; differentiate route (underline/bar/weight), tool (ghost icon button), and status (plain meta text).
Reject marketplace-hero or dashboard-stat header patterns; shopkeepers need a quiet shell and a loud fulfill action in the page, not a chrome status parade.
Do not hide Sign out or invent a new status card strip under the header — collapse, don't decorate.
